title: Synthetic Cybersecurity Onboarding
disclaimer: This is synthetic demo content created for the Agents League hackathon. It contains no real company policy, customer data, employee data, tenant content, or confidential information.

sources.title: Acceptable AI Tools Policy
sources.disclaimer: This is synthetic demo content created for the Agents League hackathon. It contains no real company policy, customer data, employee data, tenant content, or confidential information.

sections.title: Approved AI Tool Use
sections.body: Employees may use approved AI tools for work tasks when the input complies with data handling requirements.
puzzleCandidates: Select which work prompts are allowed in approved AI tools.

sections.title: Sensitive Input Restrictions
sections.body: Do not enter secrets, credentials, customer identifiers, or confidential information into public AI tools.
puzzleCandidates: Redact unsafe prompt fragments before using an AI tool.

sections.title: Human Review
sections.body: AI-generated work output requires human review before publication, customer delivery, or external sharing.
puzzleCandidates: Choose the review step before publishing AI-generated output.

sources.title: Phishing Reporting Policy
sources.disclaimer: This is synthetic demo content created for the Agents League hackathon. It contains no real company policy, customer data, employee data, tenant content, or confidential information.

sections.title: Reporting Window
sections.body: Suspected phishing messages must be reported through the security reporting portal within one hour of discovery.
puzzleCandidates: Unlock a timed hotline using the one-hour reporting threshold.

sections.title: Approved Reporting Channel
sections.body: Employees should preserve the message and avoid forwarding it outside the approved reporting workflow.
puzzleCandidates: Choose the approved portal instead of forwarding the suspicious email.

sources.title: Data Classification Policy
sources.disclaimer: This is synthetic demo content created for the Agents League hackathon. It contains no real company policy, customer data, employee data, tenant content, or confidential information.

sections.title: Classification Levels
sections.body: Information must be classified as Public, Internal, Confidential, or Restricted based on sensitivity and potential impact.
puzzleCandidates: Sort artifacts into Public, Internal, Confidential, and Restricted drawers.

sections.title: Protected Storage
sections.body: Confidential and Restricted data must be stored only in approved protected systems.
puzzleCandidates: Choose where sensitive artifacts can be stored.

sources.title: Incident Escalation Policy
sources.disclaimer: This is synthetic demo content created for the Agents League hackathon. It contains no real company policy, customer data, employee data, tenant content, or confidential information.

sections.title: Active Credential Theft
sections.body: Evidence of active credential theft requires immediate phone escalation to the security response line.
puzzleCandidates: Decide when to use phone escalation after identifying phishing evidence.

sources.title: Password And MFA Policy
sources.disclaimer: This is synthetic demo content created for the Agents League hackathon. It contains no real company policy, customer data, employee data, tenant content, or confidential information.

sections.title: MFA Requirement
sections.body: Work accounts must use multifactor authentication when accessing protected systems.
puzzleCandidates: Identify which sign-in attempt needs MFA.

sections.title: Password Reuse
sections.body: Employees must not reuse passwords across work and personal services.
puzzleCandidates: Reject a password choice that repeats a personal account password.

sources.title: Confidential Information Handling Guide
sources.disclaimer: This is synthetic demo content created for the Agents League hackathon. It contains no real company policy, customer data, employee data, tenant content, or confidential information.

sections.title: Public Tool Restrictions
sections.body: Customer records, credentials, and unreleased business information are not approved for public tools.
puzzleCandidates: Identify which prompt fragments must be removed before using an AI tool.
